queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*), AVG(k), SUM(k), MIN(k), MAX(k) FROM sbtest1
SELECT COUNT(*), AVG(k), SUM(k) FROM sbtest1 WHERE k > {threshold}
SELECT COUNT(*), AVG(k) FROM sbtest1 WHERE k BETWEEN {low} AND {high}
SELECT (k % 100) as bucket, COUNT(*), AVG(k) FROM sbtest1 GROUP BY bucket
SELECT (k % 10000) as bucket, COUNT(*), AVG(k), SUM(k) FROM sbtest1 GROUP BY bucket HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
SELECT MIN(k), MAX(k), AVG(k), STDDEV(k), VARIANCE(k) FROM sbtest1
SELECT id, k FROM sbtest1 WHERE k > {threshold} ORDER BY k DESC LIMIT 1000
SELECT id, k, c FROM sbtest1 ORDER BY k DESC LIMIT 500
SELECT (k / 10000) as range_bucket, (k % 1000) as mod_bucket, COUNT(*), MIN(k), MAX(k), AVG(k) FROM sbtest1 WHERE k > 10000 GROUP BY range_bucket, mod_bucket
queries.description: Complex aggregation with multiple grouping levels

queries.sql: SELECT (k % 5000) as bucket, COUNT(*) as cnt, AVG(k) as avg_k, SUM(k) as sum_k FROM sbtest1 GROUP BY bucket HAVING COUNT(*) > 100 ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 100
queries.description: Heavy analytical with HAVING and ORDER

queries.sql: SELECT k FROM sbtest1 ORDER BY k LIMIT 1 OFFSET {offset}
SELECT id, k, (SELECT AVG(k) FROM sbtest1 WHERE k < t.k) as running_avg FROM sbtest1 t WHERE id < 10000
SELECT a.id, a.k, b.k FROM sbtest1 a JOIN sbtest1 b ON a.k = b.id WHERE a.id < 50000 LIMIT 1000
SELECT (k % 1000) as bucket, COUNT(DISTINCT id) as distinct_count FROM sbtest1 GROUP BY bucket
SELECT (k / 1000) as k_range, (id % 100) as id_bucket, COUNT(*), AVG(k) FROM sbtest1 GROUP BY k_range, id_bucket HAVING COUNT(*) > 10
